description_long: This object is a compact, metallic shotgun with a cylindrical barrel and a short, robust receiver. The barrel appears to be made of steel, showing a slightly worn, matte finish with some discoloration near the muzzle. The receiver is blocky and features a mounting lug or attachment point on the underside. There is a visible shotgun shell positioned next to the breech, indicating the loading mechanism. The object lacks a traditional stock, making it appear more like a sawed-off or specialized breaching shotgun. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function.
description: A compact, metallic shotgun with a cylindrical barrel, short receiver, and no visible stock, featuring a utilitarian, worn finish.
description_view_0: The shotgun is viewed head-on from the muzzle, showing the circular barrel opening and a compact, cylindrical profile.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length of the barrel, the short receiver, and a separate shotgun shell aligned with the breech.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the end of the receiver and the base of the shotgun shell, emphasizing the object's short length.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the barrel, receiver, and the shell, with mounting details visible underneath.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, cylindrical, no visible ribbing or venting
